title: Customizing Tailwind CSS Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of customizing Tailwind via tailwind.config.js (theme, extend, variants).

questions.question: Which and/or what is the primarily recommended way to customize Tailwind's default theme (colors, fonts, etc.)?
options: By writing all your own CSS from scratch.
By using the tailwind.config.js file to define your design system.
By editing the tailwind.css file directly.

questions.question: What is the difference between 'theme' and 'theme.extend' in the tailwind.config.js file?
options: 'theme' overrides the default values, while 'theme.extend' adds to them without losing the defaults.
'theme' is for colors, while 'extend' is for fonts.

questions.question: Which section of the tailwind.config.js should you use to add a custom color without deleting all default colors?
Can you use official plugins like '@tailwindcss/typography' with Tailwind CSS?
options: Yes, as they provide robust, pre-built utilities for complex features (like long-form text styling).
Only in older versions of Tailwind.

questions.question: Does Tailwind customization allow you to create a completely unique design system on top of its framework foundation?
options: Yes, as the configuration file is fully programmable and infinitely flexible.
No, you can only change the colors.
